UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT VLAD COOD'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEMURRER TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED, ISSUES OF FACT ARE NOT PROPERLY RESOLVED ON THE DEMURRER; WHETHER THE E-MAIL AS A WHOLE IS A REFLECTION OF BUSINESS DISPUTE WITH USE OF HYPERBOLE IS AN ISSUE OF FACT. DEMURRER TO THE 1ST, 2ND, 4TH, 5TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED WITH FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT COOD IS A PROPER DEFENDANT. DEMURRER TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS GIVING RISE TO COOD'S LIABILITY FOR INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN HOM AND PURE. DEFENDANT, CROSS COMPLAINANT'S COUNSEL MR. D.L. RENCHER BRINGS UP REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ON PLEADINGS OF MOVING PARTY. THE COURT RESPONDS THAT IT HAS NO PROBLEM TAKING JUDICIAL NOTICE OF PLEADINGS WITH THE COURT SINCE THEY ARE ALREADY AVAILABLE FOR PUBLIC VIEWING. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: PEGGY TSUJIMOTO, CSR#5229, (415-828-1919) REPORTED. (D501)

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION TO ALLEGE SOMETHING MORE THAN BREACH OF CONTRACT. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION TO STATE FACTS CONSTITUTING OUTRAGEOUS BEHAVIOR. DEMURRER TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED, ISSUES OF FACT ARE NOT PROPERLY RESOLVED ON THE DEMURRER; WHETHER THE E-MAIL AS A WHOLE IS A REFLECTION OF BUSINESS DISPUTE WITH USE OF HYPERBOLE IS AN ISSUE OF FACT. DEMURER TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE PROPER GROUNDS FOR EJECTMENT. DEMURER TO HE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO REMOVE THE REQUEST FOR DECLARATION OF RIGHT BETWEEN COOD AND PURE.DEFENDANT, CROSS COMPLAINANT'S COUNSEL MR. D.L. RENCHER BRINGS UP REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ON PLEADINGS OF MOVING PARTY. THE COURT RESPONDS THAT IT HAS NO PROBLEM TAKING JUDICIAL NOTICE OF PLEADINGS WITH THE COURT SINCE THEY ARE ALREADY AVAILABLE FOR PUBLIC VIEWING. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: PEGGY TSUJIMOTO, CSR#5229, (415-828-1919) REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT STEPHEN HOM'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S CURRENT COMPLAINT IS UNCERTAIN AND CONFUSING. PLAINTIFF SHALL AMEND THE COMPLAINT AND CLEARLY ALLEGE (1) IN WHAT CAPACITY KATIE HOM ENTERED INTO THE LEASE/SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT - AS AN INDIVIDUAL OWNER/AGENT FOR OWNER/AS TRUSTEE OF TRUST OWNER; (2) IN WHAT CAPACITY THE CURRENTLY NAMED DEFENDANTS ARE BEING SUED - AS ORIGINAL OWNERS ON WHOSE BEHALF KATIE HOM ENTERED INTO THE AGREEMENTS; AS SUCCESSOR OWNERS/LANDLORDS TO KATIE HOM; AS AGENTS FOR THE OWNER OF THE PROPERTY; AS SUCCESSORS TO DECEDENT KATIE HOM AS INDIVIDUAL OWNER/AGENT FOR OWNER. IF PLAINTIFF PLEADS IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PLAINTIFF SHALL CLEARLY ALLEGE EACH CAPACITY OF KATIE HOM, AS CAPACITY OF THE SUCCESSORS, AND BASIS FOR LIABILITY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RONALD E. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT EVELYN HOM'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S CURRENT COMPLAINT IS UNCERTAIN AND CONFUSING. PLAINTIFF SHALL AMEND THE COMPLAINT AND CLEARLY ALLEGE (1) IN WHAT CAPACITY KATIE HOM ENTERED INTO THE LEASE/SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT - AS AN INDIVIDUAL OWNER/AGENT FOR OWNER/AS TRUSTEE OF TRUST OWNER; (2) IN WHAT CAPACITY THE CURRENTLY NAMED DEFENDANTS ARE BEING SUED - AS ORIGINAL OWNERS ON WHOSE BEHALF KATIE HOM ENTERED INTO THE AGREEMENTS; AS SUCCESSOR OWNERS/LANDLORDS TO KATIE HOM; AS AGENTS FOR THE OWNER OF THE PROPERTY; AS SUCCESSORS TO DECEDENT KATIE HOM AS INDIVIDUAL OWNER/AGENT FOR OWNER. IF PLAINTIFF PLEADS IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PLAINTIFF SHALL CLEARLY ALLEGE EACH CAPACITY OF KATIE HOM, AS CAPACITY OF THE SUCCESSORS, AND BASIS FOR LIABILITY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RONALD E. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT OLIVER PAINE DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CCP 1005 AND CCP 1013 (WHEN THE MOVING PARTY REALIZED THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANT IN HIS CAPACITY AS THE TRUSTEE HAS NOT BEEN PROPERLY SERVED, MOVING PARTY FAILED TO RE-NOTICE THE DEMURRER FOR A DATE THAT WOULD COMPLY WITH CCP 1005 AND CCC 1013) AND WITH CCP 430.41 ("IF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT, CROSS-COMPLAINT, OR ANSWER IS FILED, THE RESPONDING PARTY SHALL MEET AND CONFER AGAIN WITH THE PARTY WHO FILED THE AMENDED PLEADING BEFORE FILING A DEMURRER TO THE AMENDED PLEADING.") IF DEMURRER IS TO BE RE-FILED, MOVING PARTY IS TO IMMEDIATELY INITIATE MEET AND CONFER AND FILE A DECLARATION OF COMPLIANCE NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 28, 2019. OPPOSING PARTY TO PROMPTLY RESPOND. IF DEMURRER IS TO BE RE-FILED, IT IS TO BE FILED AND SERVED NO LATER THAN MARCH 7, 2019 ALONG WITH A MEET-AND-CONFER DECLARATION; OTHERWISE AN ANSWER SHALL BE FILED BY MARCH 7, 2019; OR IF PARTIES AGREE THAT AN AMENDED COMPLAINT WILL BE FILED, PARTIES TO APPEAR EX-PARTE TO OBTAIN AN ORDER FOR LEAVE TO AMEND ON MARCH 7, 2019. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS COMPLAINANT STEPHEN HOM'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO PROVIDE A COPY OF THE PROPOSED AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT AND FAILED TO SUBMIT A SUPPORTING DECLARATION. ADDITIONALLY, WHILE THE MOVING PARTY CLAIMS THAT THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT (TO THE EXTENT AMENDMENTS MAY BE DISCERNED FROM THE MOVING PAPERS) IS A COMPULSORY CROSS-COMPLAINT, MOVING PARTY FAILED TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT A CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST A THIRD PARTY MAY EVER BE COMPULSORY. (RUTTER GUIDE CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL 6:524). THE COURT ALSO NOTES THAT THE SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS ATTACHED TO THE REPLY PAPERS, WHILE THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES AND THE DECLARATION IN REPLY DO NOT ADDRESS THE PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS AND PROVIDE NO LAW OR EVIDENCE ON THE ISSUE. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS.) JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Cross Complainant Stephen Homs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jose Cordova, esq. (415-205-1199) for Plaintiffs /opposing party Pure Entertainment, LLC and Vlad Cood.
James Smith, esq. (415-273-4317) of Kilpatrick Townsend for Cross-Defendants/opposing party Oliver Paine and Brian Utter.
Jennifer Lim, esq. (415-877-4486) of Rencher Law Group P.C. for Defendant/Moving party Stephen Hom

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT STEPHEN HOM's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross Complaint is DENIED. Moving party failed to provide a copy of the proposed amended cross-complaint and failed to submit a supporting declaration. Additionally, while the moving party claims that the proposed amendment (to the extent amendments may be discerned from the moving papers) is a compulsory cross-complaint, moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that a cross-complaint against a third party may ever be compulsory. (Rutter Guide Civil Procedure Before Trial 6:524). The Court also notes that the Second Amended Cross-Complaint is attached to the reply papers, while the points and authorities and the declaration in reply do not address the procedural requirements and provide no law or evidence on the issue.
Order is signed in open court. (See Scanned Order for Details.) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION To Strike Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jose A. Cordova, esq. (415-205-1199) for Plaintiff/Opposing party PURE ENTERTAINMENT, LLC.
Jennifer M. Lim, esq. (415-877-4486) of Rencher Law Group PC for Defendants/Moving party Steven Hom.

***PLAINTIFF PURE ENTERTAINMENT, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions and Defendant Stephen Hom's Motion to Srike Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions are heard concurrently.***

ARGUED RE: untimely filings; availability to documents/records; sanctions. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT STEPHEN HOM's MOTION To Strike Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions is DENIED. Plaintiff's motion to compel is not untimely as Defendant filed an anti-SLAPP motion, which carries with it a statutory stay on all discovery. Additionally, as Plaintiff's separate statement "incorporates by reference" information from within the same document, there does not appear to be any violation of CRC 3.1345, which admonishes incorporating information from outside the separate statement, without fully setting forth that information in the separate statement. Order is signed in open court. (See Scanned Order for Details.) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Dec-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jose Cordova, Esq. (415-205-1199) for Plaintiff and Cross Defendant, moving party Pure Entertainment and Vlad Cood
D.L. Rencher, Esq. for Defendant, Cross Complainant and opposing party Shui King Kwon Hom also known as Katie Hom.
Court Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR#5229, (415-828-1919)

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Cross Defendant Vlad Cood's Demurrer To First Amended Cross-Complaint OVERRULED in part and SUSTAINED in part. Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action is overruled, issues of fact are not properly resolved on the demurrer; whether the e-mail as a whole is a reflection of business dispute with use of hyperbole is an issue of fact. Demurrer to the 1st, 2nd, 4th, 5th and 6th causes of action are sustained with final leave to amend to allege facts showing that Cood is a proper defendant. Demurrer to the 7th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts giving rise to Cood's liability for interference with contractual relationship between Hom and Pure. Defendant, Cross Complainant's counsel Mr. D.L. Rencher brings up request for judicial notice on pleadings of moving party. The Court responds that it has no problem taking judicial notice of pleadings with the Court since they are already available for public viewing. Prevailing party to submit order approved as to form. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR#5229, (415-828-1919) Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Jul-01-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Matt Kumin, Esq (415-655-7494) for Pure Entertainment, Plaintiff/Cross Defendant and moving party
Ryan Lester, Esq (415-877-4486) special appearance for Shui King Kwon Hom, Defendant/Cross Complainant and opposing party

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:CROSS DEFENDANT PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The demurrer is SUSTAINED as to COA 2 based on failure to allege Pure's performance was required and failure to plead facts to show any special relationship or other conduct justifying a claim of breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing, with leave to amend to plead such facts. The demurrer is OVERRULED as to COA 3, defamation. Quoted statement can reasonably be construed as defamatory per se. Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to COA 4, for failure to state facts constituting outrageous behavior, with leave to amend. Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to COA 6, declaratory relief, with leave to amend to remove requests for declaratory relief as to past duties.Prevailing party to submit order approved as to form.Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)
